skillName: Add and subtract numbers given in standard form
misconceptionSummary: Students add the A values and add the exponents, instead of converting to equal powers first.

visuals.ruleText: You can only add or subtract standard form numbers that have the same power of 10
visuals.subText: Convert both numbers to the same power first, then add the A values

steps.narration: Addition and subtraction require matching units. In standard form, the power of ten is the unit. Before adding, rewrite both numbers using the same power of ten.
Rewrite three point two times ten to the five as thirty-two times ten to the four — same value, different form. Now both share the power ten to the four and the A values can be added directly.
The result thirty-six point seven is not between one and ten — it is not in standard form. Divide by ten and increase the exponent by one: three point six seven times ten to the five.

visuals.label: Match powers → add A values → restore to standard form

steps.narration: Three point six seven times ten to the five. Match the powers, add the A values, then adjust back to standard form if needed.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: 3.2 × 10⁵ + 4.7 × 10⁴ → (3.2+4.7) × 10⁹ = 7.9 × 10⁹. You cannot add A values and exponents separately. Match the powers first, then add A values.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Never add A values and exponents independently. Rewrite both numbers using the same power of ten first — then add the A values and adjust back to standard form.
